en.name: Hematocrit in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context The hematocrit is the volume fraction of red blood cells (erythrocytes) in whole blood — i.e. the proportion of the blood volume occupied by erythrocytes [1]. It is part of every routine complete blood count (CBC) and is used to assess anaemia, polycythaemia, hydration status, and to follow blood loss or transfusion. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a haematology analyser on whole blood (EDTA tube). It is collected in routine biochemistry and intensive care, including on point-of-care blood-gas analysers that compute hematocrit from haemoglobin or measure it directly by impedance. Results are reported as a fraction, either as a percentage (e.g. **42 %**) or as a unitless ratio (e.g. **0.42** or `0.42 L/L`); the two are mathematically equivalent. ## Included Concepts The set contains fifteen LOINC "Lab Test" concepts. LOINC distinguishes two property variants for the same physical quantity: - **`[Volume Fraction]` (VFr)** — historical convention, reported in `%` or `L/L` (e.g. 42 % or 0.42 L/L). - **`[Pure volume fraction]` (VFr.DF)** — IUPAC-aligned convention introduced in 2017, reported as a dimensionless number (e.g. 0.42 with no unit). Mathematically identical to VFr. Both conventions are included because real-world datasets may use either. Within each convention, concepts vary by specimen and by measurement method. ### Volume Fraction (historical, reported in % or L/L) #### Generic blood (method not specified) - *3009542 / 20570-8 — Hematocrit [Volume Fraction] of Blood (LOINC)*: most widely used LOINC code, returned by haematology analysers when the method is not detailed. #### Method-specific - *3023314 / 4544-3 — Hematocrit [Volume Fraction] of Blood by Automated count (LOINC)*: automated impedance or optical method (most common). - *3019909 / 4545-0 — Hematocrit [Volume Fraction] of Blood by Centrifugation (LOINC)*: micro-haematocrit reference method, manual centrifugation. - *3013752 / 31100-1 — Hematocrit [Volume Fraction] of Blood by Impedance (LOINC)*: impedance-based count. - *3050746 / 48703-3 — Hematocrit [Volume Fraction] of Blood by Estimated (LOINC)*: estimated from haemoglobin (typically Hct ≈ 3 × Hb), used by some point-of-care devices. #### Specimen-specific - *3023230 / 32354-3 — Hematocrit [Volume Fraction] of Arterial blood (LOINC)*: explicit arterial sample (often from a blood-gas analyser). - *3034976 / 41654-5 — Hematocrit [Volume Fraction] of Venous blood (LOINC)*: explicit venous sample. - *3028813 / 42908-4 — Hematocrit [Volume Fraction] of Capillary blood (LOINC)*: capillary heel-prick or fingerstick (neonatal, point-of-care). - *3034037 / 41655-2 — Hematocrit [Volume Fraction] of Mixed venous blood (LOINC)*: mixed venous sample (typically from a Swan-Ganz catheter). ### Pure volume fraction (IUPAC, reported as a dimensionless number) - *1092441 / 104826-3 — Hematocrit [Pure volume fraction] of Blood (LOINC)* - *42869588 / 71833-8 — Hematocrit [Pure volume fraction] of Blood by Automated count (LOINC)* - *42869587 / 71832-0 — Hematocrit [Pure volume fraction] of Arterial blood (LOINC)* - *42869584 / 71829-6 — Hematocrit [Pure volume fraction] of Venous blood (LOINC)* - *42869585 / 71830-4 — Hematocrit [Pure volume fraction] of Mixed venous blood (LOINC)* - *42869586 / 71831-2 — Hematocrit [Pure volume fraction] of Capillary blood (LOINC)* **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows the LOINC property: - For `[Volume Fraction]` concepts: **`%`** (`8554 / % — percent (UCUM)`). LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS` is `%` for all variants. - For `[Pure volume fraction]` concepts: **`1`** (`32912 / 1 — Generic unit for indivisible thing (UCUM)`, dimensionless), aligned with NPU [2]. - `L/L` (`44777604 / L/L — liter per liter (UCUM)`) is mathematically equivalent and may be used as an alternative source unit. Conversions: `1 L/L = 100 %`, `1 % = 0.01 L/L`, `1 (dimensionless) = 100 %`. ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded by name. The following LOINC concepts that exist for hematocrit are deliberately not part of this set: - **Other specimens**: cord blood, body fluid, bone marrow, CSF, dialysis fluid, pleural fluid, synovial fluid, urine, stem cell product, fetal blood — out of scope for a "blood" concept set. - **Glucose tolerance test series** — hematocrit measured at fixed time points during an oral glucose challenge (research/study context, not routine care): - `1091805 / 104874-3 — Hematocrit [Volume Fraction] of Blood --baseline` (pre-dose, t=0) - `1092140 / 104875-0 — Hematocrit [Volume Fraction] of Blood --30 minutes post dose glucose` - `1091711 / 104876-8 — Hematocrit [Volume Fraction] of Blood --1 hour post dose glucose` - `1091224 / 104877-6 — Hematocrit [Volume Fraction] of Blood --1.5 hours post dose glucose` - `1091140 / 104878-4 — Hematocrit [Volume Fraction] of Blood --2 hours post dose glucose` - `1091362 / 104879-2 — Hematocrit [Volume Fraction] of Blood --2.5 hours post dose glucose` - `1092370 / 104880-0 — Hematocrit [Volume Fraction] of Blood --3 hours post dose glucose` - **Test status / panel concepts** — these are panel grouping or test-status codes, not measurement results: - `3039251 / 39227-4 — Hematocrit test status CPHS` - `3001643 / 24360-0 — Hemoglobin and Hematocrit panel - Blood` - `3964676 / 101655-9 — Basic metabolic and hematocrit panel - Blood` - `1260092 / 104076-5 — Basic metabolic with hemoglobin and hematocrit panel - Blood` - **Ratios** like `3010748 / 16931-8 — Hematocrit/Hemoglobin [Ratio] of Blood by Automated count` — a derived ratio, not a hematocrit value. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "hematocrit", "Ht", "Hct", "PCV" (packed cell volume) or similar without further specification: `3009542 / 20570-8 — Hematocrit [Volume Fraction] of Blood`. By far the most common LOINC code. - **Method-specific source**: if the source explicitly states the method (impedance, centrifugation, estimated), map to the corresponding method-specific concept. Otherwise, default to the generic `3009542 / 20570-8 — Hematocrit [Volume Fraction] of Blood`. - **Specimen-specific source**: if the source explicitly states "arterial", "venous", "capillary", or "mixed venous" blood, map to the corresponding specimen concept. - **Source uses the dimensionless representation** (e.g. value `0.42` without `%` or `L/L`): if the LOINC code in the source is one of the `[Pure volume fraction]` codes (e.g. `1092441 / 104826-3 — Hematocrit [Pure volume fraction] of Blood`), map to that concept. If the source uses the historical `[Volume Fraction]` codes but reports `0.42` as a fraction, map to the `[Volume Fraction]` concept and store the value with the unit `L/L` (which is the alternative supported by the unit conversions). - **Do not confuse with**: erythrocyte count (cells per volume, different concept set), haemoglobin concentration, mean corpuscular volume (MCV), or hematocrit on non-blood specimens. ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 20570-8 — Hematocrit [Volume Fraction] of Blood (Part description for "Erythrocyte"). Available: https://loinc.org/20570-8/ [2] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
